projects.title: Secure Community Taxi Fleet with In-Vehicle Surveillance System
projects.summary: Deploy a fleet of 20 dedicated taxis equipped with interior and exterior security cameras to serve Bosques de las Lomas residents. Taxis will operate on-demand through a dedicated app, with real-time GPS tracking and direct emergency contact to local security. Camera footage will be stored locally with INAI-compliant privacy protocols. This addresses safety concerns for residents traveling to/from the colonia, especially during evening hours and for vulnerable passengers. Taxis will be stationed at key pickup points including Explanada de las Palmas and main entrances on Paseo de las Palmas.

milestones.title: Regulatory Compliance and Vendor Selection
milestones.description: Obtain SEDUVI permits for camera installations, secure INAI data privacy compliance, and select taxi operators through 3-quote procurement process. Establish agreements with junta de vecinos for expanded pickup locations to support 20-vehicle fleet.

milestones.title: Phase 1: First 10 Vehicle Installations
milestones.description: Install interior/exterior cameras, GPS tracking, panic buttons, and secure data storage systems in first 10 vehicles. Configure CDMX-compliant data storage and emergency response protocols.

milestones.title: Mobile App Development and Phase 2 Installations
milestones.description: Complete remaining 10 vehicle security installations while developing resident-only booking app with real-time tracking, emergency features, and fleet management for 20 vehicles. Conduct beta testing with 30 volunteer households.

milestones.title: Full Fleet Launch and Community Integration
milestones.description: Deploy complete 20-taxi service with multiple pickup zones at Explanada de las Palmas, main entrances, and secondary locations. Train all drivers on security protocols and conduct comprehensive resident orientation through WhatsApp groups and evening meetings.

Taxis will operate on-demand through a dedicated app, with real-time GPS tracking and direct emergency contact to local security. aiBlueprint.budgetJustification: Similar taxi fleet security upgrades in CDMX cost ~MXN 35,000-40,000 per vehicle including cameras, GPS, panic buttons, and installation. With 20 vehicles at MXN 37,500 average plus app development and INAI compliance systems.

milestones.title: Regulatory Compliance and Vendor Selection

monitoringHints: Monthly review of camera footage storage compliance with INAI regulations across all 20 vehicles
Quarterly resident satisfaction surveys distributed through WhatsApp broadcast groups with fleet utilization analysis
Weekly verification of GPS tracking accuracy and emergency response system functionality for full fleet
Monthly financial audit of taxi operator contracts and fee structure transparency for 20-vehicle operation
Bi-annual review of pickup location usage data to optimize station placement for expanded fleet

demo-project-001.text: Excelente proyecto, llevan semanas con la cámara en la esquina de Palmas sin funcionar.
Ya aporté 1,000 MXN. ¿Cuándo instalan las cámaras en Presa Falcón?

demo-project-002.text: El diseño final ya fue aprobado por el arquitecto paisajista. ¡Iniciamos en 2 semanas!
